heading: Comparison Table
content:
The three most relevant alternatives a Bangladeshi family is likely to consider alongside Kholisha honey are: locally farmed multi-floral honey (the most common type sold in shops and online), other Sundarbans honey varieties (such as Keora or Goran), and imported premium honey (such as Manuka from New Zealand or Sidr from Yemen).
Dimension Kholisha Flower Honey
(Hasanah Mart)		 Local Farmed Honey
(commercial brands)		 Other Sundarbans Varieties
(Keora, Goran, Bain)		 Imported Premium Honey
(Manuka, Sidr)
Origin Wild — Sundarbans mangrove forest, Bangladesh Farmed — managed bee colonies, typically open agricultural land Wild — same Sundarbans forest, different floral source Wild or farmed — New Zealand, Yemen, or other foreign origin
Floral source Single source — Kholisha bloom only Mixed — whatever is available near farm; varies by batch Single or mixed — depends on seller and season Single source — Manuka tree or Sidr tree respectively
Processing Raw — cloth-strained, unheated Usually pasteurised and fine-filtered Varies — raw to partially processed depending on seller Varies — many imported honeys are minimally processed; some are pasteurised
Taste profile Mild, clean, gently floral — not overpowering Generic sweet; little distinctive character; varies widely Stronger, earthier notes; Keora in particular has a more robust flavour Distinctly strong — Manuka is bold and slightly medicinal; Sidr is rich and intensely sweet
Adulteration risk Moderate in the broader market; lower when sourced carefully as we do High — widespread sugar syrup blending in commercial brands Moderate to high — same market problems as Kholisha; depends heavily on seller Lower for certified products; risk exists in uncertified imports
Certification No GI certification; BSTI available with some suppliers BSTI certification common; does not verify origin No GI certification; same situation as Kholisha MGO/UMF rating for Manuka; internationally recognised and lab-verified
Price range (BDT/kg) ৳1,100 per kg jar (Hasanah Mart) ৳300 – ৳600 ৳800 – ৳1,500 depending on seller and traceability ৳2,000 – ৳5,000+ depending on grade and import route
Shelf life 2+ years stored correctly; crystallises naturally Often longer due to pasteurisation; may not crystallise Similar to Kholisha if raw; longer if processed Long shelf life; certified grades have expiry dating
Local availability Available year-round via Hasanah Mart, delivered to your door Widely available in shops and online Available but harder to find from reliable sellers Available through import retailers and some online stores

heading: When to Choose Kholisha Honey
content:
Kholisha honey from Hasanah Mart is a good fit for your family if:

**You want to know where your food comes from.** This honey has a clear, specific origin — a named forest, a documented harvesting tradition, a traceable supply chain. That is not something most honey on the market can offer.

**You prefer food that is minimally processed.** Raw, unheated, cloth-strained — it reaches you as close to its natural state as practical. If your family avoids heavily processed foods, this is consistent with that.

**You want a mild, everyday honey.** Kholisha has a gentle flavour that works well for daily use — in tea, with food, or on its own. It is not sharp or overpowering, which makes it easy for the whole family including children over 1 year old.

**You want genuine Bangladeshi origin without paying an import premium.** You get wild-harvested, single-source, raw honey — the same qualities that make imported premium honey expensive — at a fraction of the price, and produced right here at home.

**You shop with Hasanah Mart for convenience.** We deliver it fresh to your door. No hunting around for a trustworthy seller, no uncertainty about what you are getting.

</section>

heading: When Another Option May Be Better
heading_path: When Another Option May Be Better
content:
We would rather be honest with you than oversell.

**Choose local farmed honey if budget is the primary concern.** If your family needs honey purely as a sweetener and price is the deciding factor, farmed honey at ৳300–৳600/kg does the job. Just buy from a brand with BSTI certification and be aware that adulteration is common at the lower end of the market.

**Choose Keora or Goran honey if you prefer a stronger, bolder flavour.** Some families — particularly those who use honey in cooking or prefer a more intense taste — find Kholisha too mild. Keora honey from the Sundarbans has a more robust, distinctive character. The sourcing challenges are similar, so apply the same scrutiny when buying.

**Choose certified Manuka or Sidr honey if you need internationally verified quality for a specific purpose.** These honeys carry internationally recognised grading systems (MGO/UMF for Manuka) that are independently lab-tested and legally protected. If you are looking for a honey with a certified and verified potency level — for example, for someone in your family with a specific health-related reason advised by a doctor — certified imported honey offers verification that domestic honey currently cannot match. The cost is significantly higher, and you will need to import or buy through a verified retailer.

**A straightforward summary:**

| Your Priority | Better Choice |
|---|---|
| Lowest price | Local farmed honey (BSTI certified) |
| Bold, strong flavour | Keora or Goran Sundarbans honey |
| International lab certification | Certified Manuka or Sidr |
| Raw, traceable, local, everyday use | Kholisha honey from Hasanah Mart |

</section>
